title: Dynamic Batch Mixing in Multi-Task SFT
concept: Using dynamic batch mixing ensures every training batch contains a representative sample of all target tasks, keeping weight updates stable.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary goal of Multi-Task SFT?

options.text: To make the AI run faster on old smartphones.
To teach the AI multiple different skills at the same time.
To delete unnecessary data from the model.

exercises.explain: In Multi-Task SFT (Supervised Fine-Tuning), we want the model to learn multiple skills at the same time.

cards.title: The Multi-Task Forgetfulness Problem
cards.body: If you train an AI on coding all morning, then math all afternoon, it will likely forget how to code by lunchtime! How do we teach an AI multiple skills at the same time without it forgetting? We mix them up dynamic-style!

cards.title: What is Dynamic Batch Mixing?
cards.body: Dynamic Batch Mixing is a training technique where every single mini-batch contains a carefully blended percentage of data from different tasks (like 40% math, 40% writing, and 20% coding), rather than training on one task at a time.

cards.title: The Party Snack Mix
cards.body: Imagine you are preparing a multi-flavor party snack bowl. If you dump all the pretzels in first, then all the chips, and then all the M&Ms, guests will only get one snack at a time. Dynamic batch mixing is like tossing the bowl continuously as you pour, so every single handful gives you a perfect, tasty mix of all three snacks.

cards.title: Dynamic Mixing in Action
cards.body: Suppose we want an AI to learn translation, summarization, and coding. Instead of giving it a batch of 100 coding prompts, we build a dynamic batch of 50 translation prompts, 30 summarization prompts, and 20 coding prompts. The AI learns all three skills in every single update step.

cards.title: Myth: One Task at a Time is Best
cards.body: People often think you should train an AI on Task A completely before moving to Task B. In reality, doing this causes the AI to forget Task A. Mixing the tasks together in every batch is much better for long-term memory!

cards.title: Visualizing the Mix
cards.body: Compare how training batches look with and without dynamic mixing. Notice how the mixed approach prevents the AI from forgetting.

right.text: Batch 1: 50% Math + 50% Coding. Batch 2: 50% Math + 50% Coding. (Stable learning!)

left.text: Batch 1: 100% Math. Batch 2: 100% Coding. (AI forgets Math during Batch 2)

exercises.prompt: What happens if we train a multi-task AI on only one task type for too long without mixing?

options.text: The training data will become corrupt and unreadable.
The AI's weights will drift too far toward the active task, forgetting the others.
The AI will automatically shut down due to overheating.
The AI's vocabulary size will instantly double.

exercises.explain: Training on only one task at a time causes the AI's weights to shift too far in one direction, forgetting previous tasks. Dynamic mixing keeps weights balanced.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Dynamic batch mixing helps prevent the AI from forgetting one task while it learns another.
exercises.explain: Dynamic batch mixing ensures every batch has representation from all target tasks to keep training stable.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing training concept below:
exercises.sentence: To keep weight updates stable across many tasks, we use ________ to blend tasks into every step.
exercises.explain: Dynamic batch mixing combines different tasks into each batch to prevent the AI from forgetting.

exercises.prompt: Which AI training setup is using dynamic batch mixing?

options.text: An AI that is trained on 10,000 math equations on Monday, and then 10,000 poems on Tuesday.
An AI trained on batches that always contain 5 math equations, 3 logic puzzles, and 2 creative writing prompts.
An AI trained on a single massive file containing only historical dates.

exercises.explain: The AI in the second example is demonstrating dynamic batch mixing because it receives a balanced, representative slice of multiple tasks (math, logic, writing) in a single update step.

exercises.prompt: You are deploying a multi-task assistant. How does dynamic batch mixing help keep the AI's training stable?

options.text: It keeps weight updates stable by putting a representative mix of all tasks in every single batch.
It runs one task completely to 100% completion before starting the next one.
It trains only on the easiest task to save computer processing power.
It randomly deletes half of the training data to speed up the process.

exercises.explain: Dynamic batch mixing maintains a stable ratio of different tasks in every single step, preventing the model's weights from drifting or forgetting previous tasks.
